title: Lebanon's Shiites, torn over Hezbollah, question the meaning of their sacrifices
subhead: Days after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement, supporters and opponents of the militia-party are still measuring the consequences of the devastating war.

Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
subhead: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

title: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France, a sudden move with far-reaching consequences
subhead: Captain Ibrahim Traoré announced on June 26 that Burkina Faso will break off bilateral relations with Paris. The 2,500 French nationals living in the country will no longer be able to rely on embassy or consular services, as staff are being repatriated.

title: Nearly 100 players born in France were selected for the 2026 World Cup – but only 23 play for France
subhead: It's a testament to France's success as a breeding ground for international football talent. And sometimes, France misses out on promising dual-nationality players, like Morocco's Ayyoub Bouaddi.

title: 250 years of American independence: In 1976, a bicentennial celebrated with patriotism despite divisions
subhead: 'America 250' (10/13). In 1976, the United States turned a page on one of the most turbulent chapters in its history. After the Vietnam War and the resignation of Richard Nixon, the bicentennial offered Americans a moment of relief. Minority rights, social justice – new debates continued to stir society, notably led by the economist Jeremy Rifkin and the 'red, white and blue' left.

Monaco explosion: The Ukrainian-born oligarch's shady business dealings
subhead: Vadym Yermolaiev, a wealthy businessman, was targeted by a homemade explosive bomb on Monday. Despite renouncing his Ukrainian nationality, he continued business activities connected to his country of origin.
byline: Lucas Minisini,  Sofia Fischer and  Thomas d’Istria

title: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
subhead: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

title: From 'meat grinder' to 'ransom': The key words that define 'Russian soldiers' daily life
subhead: Anthropologist Alexandra Arkhipova has compiled an 'ethnographic dictionary of war' after analyzing some 7,000 letters retrieved from the website of the Russian human rights commissioner. Published in Russian on the independent exile media site 'Echo,' her work reveals the language and suffering of military life.
